summary: Backend-only testing for v3.6.13 TCP Port Fingerprint Probe feature. Created /app/backend/tests/test_tcp_port_fingerprint.py with 5 pytest test cases (persistence of ip_port_probes in db.network_discovery, discovered_endpoints listening_ports population, topology node enrichment + type classification, direct _guess_endpoint_type unit test, legacy payload regression). Created a dedicated TEST_ client + seeded device_poll_status via pymongo, cleaned up on teardown. All 5 tests passed 100%.
critical_code_review_comments: In routes/connector.py connector_network_discovery the `managed_ips` set is built from device_macs.ip AND device_poll_status.device_ip. This means endpoints for IPs that are managed are flagged is_managed=True and then filtered out of the topology (endpoints query is `is_managed: False`). Listening_ports for managed devices are persisted in discovered_endpoints but are NOT exposed on the managed node in topology (only non-managed leaf nodes carry listening_ports). If product intent is to also badge managed devices with their TCP fingerprint in topology, this needs an extra enrichment pass on managed nodes.
ip_ports_map keys strictly on IP; if the same IP appears in multiple probes only the last one wins (dict overwrite). Consider merging ports across duplicate IPs.

listening_ports is stored as `sorted(set(int(p) for p in ports if str(p).isdigit()))` - non-digit ports (e.g. strings like '22/tcp') are silently dropped. Acceptable but could be logged for observability.

routes/topology.py _guess_endpoint_type has dead code branch (lines 704-706): computes `ports & {443,80,8080}` but does nothing with the result (just `pass`). Either remove or implement the firewall/appliance classification.
seed_data_creation: Created a temporary TEST_tcp_fp_<uuid> client via POST /api/clients and inserted one managed switch into device_poll_status (IP 10.77.77.1). All test data is cleaned up in fixture teardown (network_discovery, discovered_endpoints, mac_connections, port_speeds, device_poll_status, topology_layouts, alerts, port_flap_events, and the client itself).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Feature v3.6.13 TCP Port Fingerprint verified end-to-end on backend. Test file is /app/backend/tests/test_tcp_port_fingerprint.py and relies on python-dotenv to load /app/backend/.env + /app/frontend/.env so MONGO_URL, DB_NAME and REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL are set for both pymongo and requests. Existing connector test (test_connector_endpoints.py) uses the 86BIT_Office real client api_key=noc_35cf39b4d68740b1a981aedef2ee293d; the new test uses its own isolated TEST_ client to avoid polluting real data.
rca of the issue: N/A - feature test, all assertions pass. Verified: (1) ip_port_probes persists in db.network_discovery, (2) discovered_endpoints have listening_ports populated from ip_ports_map when ep_ip matches probe IP, empty list otherwise, (3) topology nodes of role=discovered_endpoint expose listening_ports + type='server' for [3389], 'printer' for [9100], 'server' for [22,80], (4) _guess_endpoint_type('', '', [3389]) → 'server', [9100] → 'printer', [22,80] → 'server', [5060] → 'generic', hostname overrides ports, (5) legacy payload without ip_port_probes returns 200 and yields empty listening_ports list on endpoints.
